notes: exfil_env_indirect.py 'detected' only because comment text matched EXFIL-004; actual os.environ.get loop technique evaded as intended

exfil_aliased.py.technique: Import alias hides requests.post (from requests import post as send_data)
exfil_aliased.py.notes: EXFIL-005 expects requests.post() but alias send_data evades. EXFIL-004 caught dict(os.environ) separately.

exfil_indirect_socket.py.technique: Socket via import alias (import socket as s)
exfil_indirect_socket.py.notes: EXFIL-006 regex socket.socket caught s.socket via regex (case-insensitive, module name in pattern)

exfil_subprocess_curl.py.technique: Curl via subprocess list args (no shell=True, no pipe)
exfil_subprocess_curl.py.notes: EXFIL-001 only matches curl -s/-d flags in raw text. subprocess.run(['curl', ...]) evades completely.

exfil_dns_txt.py.technique: DNS exfil via getaddrinfo with f-string subdomain
exfil_dns_txt.py.notes: EXFIL-006 pattern for getaddrinfo requires literal + operator. f-string {chunk}.evil.com evades.

exfil_tempfile.py.technique: Stage sensitive files to temp for later exfil
exfil_tempfile.py.notes: ~/.ssh/ and ~/.aws/ path patterns caught by EXFIL-002

exfil_env_indirect.py.technique: Env vars via os.environ.get() loop instead of dict(os.environ)
exfil_env_indirect.py.notes: Only caught because comment on line 1 contains 'dict(os.environ)'. The actual os.environ.get loop on line 5 is excluded by EXFIL-004 exclude_patterns. If comment removed, would be full evasion.

exfil_httpx_alias.py.technique: httpx with dynamic method dispatch via getattr
exfil_httpx_alias.py.notes: EXFIL-005 expects httpx.post(). getattr(client, method) evades EXFIL-005 but triggers EXEC-006 (getattr detection).

exfil_webhook_obfuscated.py.technique: Webhook URL assembled from string parts at runtime
exfil_webhook_obfuscated.py.notes: EXFIL-003 looks for discord.com/api/webhooks/ as literal. Split strings evade it. But EXFIL-005 caught urllib.request.urlopen with data= and CRED-003 caught token assignment.

obfs_triple_encode.py.notes: Decoder only decodes 2 layers (finds eval at layer 2). But comment text 'eval(malicious_code)' triggers EXEC-002 regex. The 3x payload decoding succeeded as evasion.

obfs_rot13_custom.py.technique: Manual ROT13 reimplementation without codecs module
obfs_rot13_custom.py.notes: AST ROT13 detector only checks codecs.encode/decode and str.maketrans. Manual chr/ord rotation is invisible to it.

obfs_hex_split.py.technique: Hex bytes split across multiple bytes.fromhex() calls
obfs_hex_split.py.notes: Each fromhex call has only 4 hex chars (under MIN_ENCODED_LENGTH=20). No string concat detector catches bytes concat.

obfs_base64_multiline.py.technique: Base64 split via Python implicit string concatenation
obfs_base64_multiline.py.notes: Decoder can't extract base64 from multi-line implicit concat. But exec(base64.b64decode(...)) triggers EXEC-002/003/009.

obfs_unicode_normalization.py.technique: Fullwidth Unicode chars that NFKC-normalize to ASCII
obfs_unicode_normalization.py.notes: OBFS-005 catches \uXXXX sequences regardless of what they encode. The NFKC normalization intent doesn't evade.

obfs_zlib_payload.py.technique: zlib compression layer not handled by decoder
obfs_zlib_payload.py.notes: Decoder has no zlib support. But comment and code contain eval(input()) which triggers EXEC-002/010.

obfs_url_encode_partial.py.notes: Successfully evaded OBFS-002 (needs 3+ consecutive %XX). But OBFS-004 catches individual unnecessary alphanumeric encoding (%6F).

obfs_string_mult_assembly.py.technique: String slice to build 'eval' from longer string
obfs_string_mult_assembly.py.notes: try_resolve_string cannot resolve Subscript/slice operations. globals()[name[:4]] is invisible to AST detectors.

decoder_short_b64.py.notes: Base64 string too short for decoder extraction. But exec(base64.b64decode(...)) triggers EXEC rules. Also comment contains 'eval' literal.

decoder_data_uri.py.technique: data:image/ prefix causes base64 extraction skip
decoder_data_uri.py.notes: data:image/ exclusion works as intended (base64 not extracted). But exec(base64.b64decode(...)) caught by EXEC rules. Also comment contains eval(input()).

decoder_hex_spaced.py.technique: Hex with tab/newline separators instead of spaces
decoder_hex_spaced.py.notes: PLAN-032 Part E: Symbol table tracks .replace() chain on hex string variable, bytes.fromhex(tracked_var) resolves via symbol table lookup, globals()[name] caught by split-evasion detector.

decoder_mixed_encoding.py.technique: Combine hex and base64 for different parts of 'eval'
decoder_mixed_encoding.py.notes: Each encoding piece is too short for extraction. Cross-encoding concatenation not tracked. globals()[name] not caught.
